sections.paragraph:
οἰϰονόμος, ου, ὁ [οἶκος, νέμω ‘manage’] ‘one who takes care of (business) affairs’, esp. involving accounts in a large household

—a. one responsible for an estate and its financial structure,manager Lk 12:42; 16:1, 3, 8; 1 Cor 4:2; Gal 4:2.

—b. by ext.

—α. of a chief financial officer in a city, which is viewed as a household, manager, treasurer Ro 16:23.

—β. of Paul as God’s appointed manager 1 Cor 4:1; Tit 1:7; 1 Pt 4:10.
